post.post_content:
[:en]
[Phnom Penh] On October 26,2023, Family Care First project hosted the 22nd Learning Summit at Himawari Hotel. The main objective of event updated on progress at national and sub-national levels, updated on FCF project implementation and shared the research on “Toward their Full Potential” study results.
In the speech, H.E Din Borey, General Director of Social Service Department, MoSVY highlighted that in order to ensure the implementing of project successfully, effectively and efficiently in the purpose of promoting well-being and serve the best interest of children, he urged each member of meeting, especially the implementing partners to monitor and lay out action plan aligned with child protection sector 2022-2026 and action plan of enhancing the alternative care 2023-2027 as well as other strategic documents issued by the ministry. At the same time, H.E Din Borey would like to thank the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) that has financially supporting for the implementation of Family Care First (FCF) since 2015 until the end of December of 2023 and extending until 2025. The implementation of Family Care First (FCF) has made a significant contribution to the development of child protection sector in Cambodia as well as the strengthening of the social protection system through the direct provision of services to children, families, and communities.
Ms. Edith Bennema, Chief of Party FCF, highlighted one of the underlying principles of the project to contribute to strengthening the child protection system and the capacity of government at national and sub-national level, and civil society to implement care reform has been to encourage shared learning and evidence-based advocacy and program design. FCF has had an impact at the policy level and has delivered direct services to families. Today, however, she would like to highlight some events that have had an impact on strengthening organizational capacities. These include:
• 227 Quarterly partner visits
• 40 Learning events such as 22 Learning Summits, and 10 community of Practice and eight Learning Exchange Visits
• 89 Bulletins
• Training for partners and network members on a variety of topics such as social work, case management, monitoring and evaluation, and local tax laws.
The project has supported 24 organizations – 75 per cent local organizations - to implement 28 projects since the start of the project in nine provinces. This collaboration has resulted in impact on an individual level:
• Nearly 500 children and youth reintegrated with biological families.
• More than 400 children placed in kinship and foster care – a relatively new form of alternative care that the project helped develop and implement in Cambodia.
• Nearly 45,000 beneficiaries have received support services through a case management approach.
• 39 residential care institutions (RCI) engaged in the process of transition or closure. Among that, 12 RCIs have closed. As an evidence-based organization, we have analyzed the challenges and reviewed the learning of the past years and are suggesting an alternative approach.
There were 70 participants to joint this event who were H.E Din Borey, General Director of the Social Service Department of MoSVY, Ms. Alyssa Moy, representative of USAID, Mr. Hong Reaksmey, Country Director of Save the Children in Cambodia, Ms. Edith Bennema, Chief of Party FCF, H.E Representative from National Social Assistant Fund (NSAF) of MoSVY, H.E Chairman for Provincial Women Consultative Council for Women and Children, DoSVY Directors and representative from FCF network partners.
[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Family Care First project hosted the 22nd Learning Summit[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]
Phnom Penh: On September 27, 2023, Family Care First Project conducted a Validation Workshop for the Meta-review research/studies of Family-Based Care in Cambodia with the participation of representatives of Siem Reap and Battambang Provincial Department of Child Protection of MoSVY, and FCF network members including, BCN, HAGAR, M’lup Russey, OiC, Damnok Toek, TLC, Holt, CCT, UNICEF and the USAID representatives.
The objectives of the Meta-Review are to assess the relevance and use of the presented recommendations for the organization and for the society as a whole and assess what kind of studies on the issue of prevention of children being separated from their families and reintegration of children from RCI into family-based care.
The purpose of the workshop was to present the preliminary findings with Q&A session and discuss selected findings and/or conclusions to reach consensus among the network members.
[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Validation Workshop for the Meta-review research/studies of Family-Based Care in Cambodia[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Phnom Penh: On September 25-26, 2023, Family Care First (FCF) project cooperated with terre des hommes to host Learning Exchange Visit and Community of Practice at Himawari meeting hall in Phnom Penh city. The event was joined by 14 organizations with 40 participants including representatives of Cambodia National Council for Children, USAID, AusCam Freedom Project, ChildSafe, APLE, Komar Rikreay Association, Plan International, OIC Cambodia, Safe Haven, Damnok Toek, Children’s Future International (CFI), Holt International Cambodia, Hagar International Foundation, SKO, CCT, TLC, ChildFund Cambodia, COLT, Terre des hommes, M’Lop Tapang, Mother’s Heart, DT, WHI. FCF project funded by USAID and Save the Childern in Cambodia. The purpose of this event were:

- shared and learnt from the available evidence frequency of and types of Online Child Sexual Exploitation (OCSE), prevention, referral mechanism, and interventions.

- presented National Action Plan to Prevent and Respond to OCSE in Cambodia 2021-2025, National Guideline Online Child Protection and Cambodia Child Online Protection Guideline for the Digital Technology Industry.

- learnt about good practices on how the existing mechanisms (close collaboration among stakeholders) to respond to OCSE cases.

As a result of this meeting, all participants have learnt about the child safety and child rights on online program, models of child sexual abuse and prevention measures presented by Terre des hommes, AusCam and APLE, and group discussions to find out about the impact of child online abuse, child vulnerabilities on the online system, especially the provision of support to protect children from the abuse. Participants also received information about interventions from Cambodia National Council for Children (CNCC) and the Ministry of Posts and Telecommunications on the above topics as well.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]FCF hosted Learning Exchange Visit  and Community of Practice[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Phnom Penh: The OSCaR (Open-Source Case Management and Record-Keeping) System is a web-based system that enables accurate information gathering and customizable program modeling from a central hub. Child development and program outcomes can be easily monitored, making social work practices, data collection, and data analysis simpler and more reliable. Thanks to CIF’s partnership with Family Care First, OSCaR is available for free to NGOs within Cambodia that are seeking to strengthen families and improve their social work practice. It is also available for a fee to organizations in other countries. In 2023, 35 organizations are using OSCaR in Cambodia.

From September 6–8, 2023, CIF provided an introduction to three international organizations (Global Renewal, Blue Dragon, and First Compassion) who are potentially interested in applying the system. The organizations are currently deciding whether to sign up.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Sharing child protection information management system OSCaR with other NGOs[:]

post.post_content: [:en]Siem Reap Province: On July 13, 2023, FCF support, TLC organized a field monitoring visit with Siem Reap Provincial Women’s and Children’s Consultative Committees (PWCCC) to two of TLC’s core projects. H.E Tes Chankiri, Deputy of WCCC, and 15 participants from representative of DoSVY, targeted DoSASW and CCWC, village, FCF, Savong Organization Cambodia (SOC) and TLC shared implementation progress and challenges and discussed way forward. DoSVY accepted the request from TLC to intensify support to transition and/or close RCIs. FCF representative encouraged TLC and its partner to raise their concern or challenge if they need support from FCF or MoSVY. WCCC acknowledged the impressive achievements of the project expressed appreciation for the manner in which the FCF project works local authorities. The WCCC Deputy asked village chiefs, CCWC and DoSASW to work closely with TLC, and to continue supporting family with re-integrated children, especially with accessing government service, and cash transfers.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]PWCCC Field Monitoring Visit[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Phnom Penh: On June 29, 2023, Save the Children International had the pleasure to announce that we have renewed a three-year collaboration agreement with the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation (MoSVY). This partnership will enable us to work closely with MoSVY on three major projects, i.e. SIDA CSO Strengthening, Promoting the Protection and Inclusion of Children Discriminated due to their Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity/Expression and Sex Characteristics/SOGIESC in Cambodia (SOGIESC), and Eliminate Violence Against Children in School (EVACiS) project.

In this partnership, our goal is to promote the rights of children as stipulated in the United Nations Convention on the Right of the Child (UNCRC) in the Kingdom of Cambodia, particularly the rights of the most vulnerable children and victims of violence, abuse, and neglect.  While contributing to the implementation of the strategic plan of the Royal Government, notably the Child Protection Sector Strategic Implementation Plan of MoSVY.

“These projects are designed to enhance the quality of life for all children, especially those who are vulnerable due to their sexual orientation, gender identity, disabilities, minority or disadvantaged backgrounds,” according to Hong Reaksmey, the Country Director of Save the Children in Cambodia, “we work collaboratively with stakeholders, and partner organizations at national and sub-national levels to develop joint plans and implement activities that address the unique needs of these children.”[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Signing Projects Agreement[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]The Community of Practice as combine with the Learning Exchange Visit on June 14, 2023, in Battambang province. Komar Rikreay Organization (KMR), Holt and CCT, hosted this learning event. The 35 participants from 14 FCF network members and partners, were divided into small groups to visit the project’s beneficiaries. The purpose of the visit was to gain more understanding on how kinship and foster care is implemented within the target province.

The participants have increased their understanding of the foster parent selection process, best practices, similarities, and differences of the kinship and foster care models implemented by the three organizations. At the end of the visit, all participants gathered for a reflection session to share their comments and recommendations on the program’s implementation.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]FCF Project team and Partners Conduct Learning Exchange Visit[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Battambang Province: On June 13, 2023, Komar Rikreay Organization (KMR), in collaboration with Holt and CCT, conducted a Community of Practice (CoP) at KMR’s meeting hall in Battambang province, with the active participation of DoSVY. Thirty-five participants from 14 organizations, including representatives of DoSVY Battambang, representatives NGO from HOLT, CCT, KMR, LWD, CFI, Damnok Toek PPT, ICS-PP, Safe Haven, SKO, PAflD, TLC, VOICE, M.H.O, took part in this learning opportunity. The purpose of this event was:
  • To share and learn from the kinship and foster Care models implemented by Komar Rikreay (KMR), HOLT, and Cambodian Children Trust (CCT).
  • To discuss the implications of kinship and foster care practices and adaptation for further expansion based on new policies, Prakas and new forms for FCF network.
As a result of the meeting, all participants learned about different models of kinship and foster care programs by Holt, CCT and KMR, engaged in group discussions on how their organization could provide support to kinship and foster care, and discussed how they could provide further support, especially children with disabilities. The participants also learned about the Decree (Prakas) on procedures to implement alternative care, as well as the Decree on kinship care and foster care presented by representative of DoSVY Battambang.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]FCF Project team and Partners Conduct the Community of Practice[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Save the Children in Cambodia hosted Mr. Steve Miller, SCI’s Global Director of Child Protection, for two days, June 08-09, 2023. Mr. Miller learned about the country’s child protection program, including FCF network members’ and sub-grant partners’ work to support the national and sub-national government, the Social Service Work Force strengthening efforts, contributions to policy and legal framework development, and critical achievements in support of vulnerable children and their families, including children with disabilities.

Mr. Miller visited a parent group in Kampong Chhnang province, where he observed the social service workforce capacity building cascaded down to the grassroots level and the direct benefit to children and families. During the trip, Mr. Miller met with implementing partners and focal point persons of the Commune Council for Women and Children (CCWC), community volunteers, and parents of children with disabilities from eight communes. Organization to Improve Communication and Swallowing Therapy Services in Cambodia (OIC), one of FCF’s funded partners, conducted a coaching/mentoring session with CCWC members who are being trained to support their communities with identifying and assisting children – and their families - with swallowing and communication difficulties. Mr. Miller observed the session and expressed his respect for the OIC’s efforts and congratulated the organization on working at all administrative levels to strengthen the system.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]SCI’s Global Director of Child Protection Visit SCI’s Cambodia Program[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Phnom Penh: On May 31, 2023, the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans, and Youth Rehabilitation (MoSVY) celebrated World Social Work Day 2023 with the theme: "Respecting Diversity Through Joint Social Action”. The World Social Work Day aims to celebrate and share the achievements of the social work sector in providing social servic

es, encouraging social development, and responding to the actual needs of vulnerable citizens, including children at risk of violence, neglect, abuse, and children with disabilities.

H.E Nim Thoth, Permanent Secretary of State, on behalf of H.E Vong Sauth, the Minister of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation, stated that Cambodia needs a strong and capable social services workforce (SSWF) to contribute to social development and community development. He highlighted that SSWF has been important in supporting and promoting the well-being of children, families, victims, and vulnerable groups.

In his opening remarks, Mr. Hong Reaksmey, Country Director of Save the Children International (SCI), emphasized the contribution of Family Care First (FCF) Project, focusing on the project’s support to strengthen Social Service Workforce (SSWF) capacity in close collaboration with the National Institute of Social Affairs, the Ministry of Interior, and UNICEF. This coalition of partners has developed the training curriculum and modules and is now conducting national-level Training of Trainer (ToT) and rolling out the training to social workers at sub-national levels. Mr. Reaksmey emphasized that FCF network members are here to support the government to ensure that the best possible social work services and support to vulnerable children and families are provided. FCF Project has been working closely with NISA on Today’s event is a recognition of a cohesive community for a powerful action for positive social change and inclusion.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]World Social Work Day 2023[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]Phnom Penh: from April 25-27 and 03 May, 2023, Family Care First (FCF) project, in cooperation with MoSVY and National Institution of Social Affair (NISA), conducted training with 32 para professional social service providers from Kandal. The training aimed to increase the capacity of the trainees to deliver social service to the most vulnerable children and adults in Cambodia, including children living with disabilities. This training is a first step in a nation-wide effort to strengthen the capacity of social service providers at national, provincial, district and commune level under the leadership of MoSVY, technical support from NISA, FCF/SCI, and UNICEF, with financing from USAID. The pre-test and post-test results indicated an average increase in knowledge from 31 to 81 percent. This level 1 consists of 9 modules are:
  1. Concept of Social Support,
  2. Ecological System Model Theory & Maslow’s Hierarchy
  3. Group Work Model
  4. Community Organization
  5. Supervision for Social Service Workforce
  6. SW and Child Protection in Humanitarian Action
  7. Engagement Skills
  8. Communication Skills
  9. Social Service Coordination, Networking, and Advocacy Skills
  We are grateful for USAID continued support to this pivotal effort to improve social services delivery and congratulate the trainees on completing these modules. We wish them luck in applying the newly gained knowledge.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Roll-out Training on Social Service Workforce level 1 at Sub-national Pilot for Kandal Province[:]

post.post_content: [:en] MoSVY and the Family Care First project organized a monitoring visit in Battambang Province from April 24-27,2023. The visit aimed to assess FCF sub-grant partners’ progress with the implementation of projects in Battambang, and identify and discuss challenges and a way forward. Eight representatives from MoSVY participated in the visit, namely H.E Touch Channy, Director General Department of Social Service Department, H.E Yeab Malyno, Director General Department of Social Policy, Mr. Phi No, Director of Child Protection Department and colleagues, and H.E Phavann Sakkeuat, Deputy Director of Provincial of DoSVY, and FCF team and FCF partners including Holt International Cambodia, CCT and CFI. CCT manages a project for RCI Care leavers who presented an impressive array of awareness raising activities undertaken in communities and on-line, as well as the critically important peer support activities conducted on a weekly basis. CFI presented the successes and challenges of engaging RCIs to transition and/or close and reunite children with their biological families. RCI transition is a complex matter as it involves many stakeholders who all need to understand why transition is necessary and how it benefits the children. MoSVY indicated its appreciation for the efforts and invited CFI to reach out in case support is needed. Finally, Holt presented progress with foster care and kinship care project. MoSVY was able to visit a family who has provided safe family-based care for a little boy for the past three years. Holt mentioned that permanency planning, e.g. domestic adoption, is a very complex and cumbersome process. MoSVY was pleased to share with the participants that under its leadership Holt and other partners were working on improving guidance on domestic adoption. We would like to thank MoSVY for the recommendations and appreciation expressed during the visit, and we are grateful to our partners for the time and energy invested in making the MoSVY jont monitoring visit a success.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]MoSVY Field Monitoring Visit in Battambang Province[:]

post.post_content: [:en] Family Care First (FCF) hosted a Co-Design Sustainability Workshop from March 22-24, 2023 in Phnom Penh to obtain input from the government, civil society and donors on the final phase of the project. The final phase (January 2024-September 2025) will focus on the localization and sustainability of the USAID investment in coordination and learning over the past eight years. The last phase also has three thematic focus areas for sub-grants: 1) Prevention of family separation; 2) Residential Care Institutions transition, closure and re-integration of residents; and 3) Alternative family-based care and permanency planning. Partners will maintain interventions addressing the needs of children living with disabilities and their families. The workshop was attended by a total of 61 participants, 25 of whom women, from Government, USAID, UNICEF and 18 NGOs, i.e. CCT, CFI, CIF, Damnok Toek, DT, FI, FSC, HAGA, HOLT, ICS-SP, LWD, KRA, MRO, SKO, OIC, TCL, WOMAN and WVI. FCF obtained valuable inputs that will shape the interventions starting January 2024.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]FCF Co-Design Sustainability Workshop[:]

post.post_content: [:en]    The Family Care First (FCF) project is a network of organizations financially supported by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) and Save the Children. On February 28, 2023, the 21st Family Care First Learning Summit was organized and held at the Himawari Hotel in Phnom Penh. There were 96 participants (40 women), including representatives of the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation, Ministry of Interior, Ministry of Information, Ministry of Labour, Ministry of Education, Youth, and Sport, the Department of Social Affairs, the WCCC, NGO Partners, development partners, and researcher. In the remark of Ms. Alyssa Moy, Education Officer, USAID Cambodia, she would like to thanks FCF network partners for all their hard work and commitment toward the implementation of the care reform in Cambodia. More children have received case management support, more children have been taken out of RCIs and placed either with their families or in kinship or foster care, more RCIs are seriously considering transition or closure, and more care leavers have had a chance to meet and support each other. She emphasized that USAID played an important role committed and supported Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehibition and FCF partners with the finalization of two studies, the Lessons Learned on Residential Care Institution Closure and the Kinship Care and Foster Care study. There are two findings in the study that is particularly relevant for this Summit.  Together we have entered our eighth year of collaboration and she believe that together we have made substantial progress in making care reform a reality. USAID support with you have all provided to the development of the child protection workplans at the national and sub-national level. These are important documents that ensure unity in vision. Take this opportunity USAID pleased announced 3 millions USD grands for 21-months extension of family care first that is good work to make Cambodia a better place for the children that are at risk of losing or do not have the loving and nurturing are of their families. [:]
post.post_title: [:en]USAID Cambodia Announced 3 Millions USD Grants for 21 Months Extension of Family Care First Project to Make Cambodia a Better Place for the Children[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]On March 06, 2023, Mr. Hong Reaksmey, Country Director of Save the Children paid a courtesy visit to  H.E. Vong Sauth, Minister of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation .

In this meeting, Mr. Reaksmey informed H.E. Vong Sauth on the works of Save the Children in the field of social affairs, including; early childhood programs that focused on positive parenting and education, child rights governance and child protection programs, education, child poverty reduction programs that focused on improving family livelihoods an social humanitarian assistance, including social protection and social assistance.

Moreover, Mr. Reaksmey informed H.E. Vong Sauth on the 21-months extension of the Family Care First Project with from January 1, 2024 to September 2025, funded by the USAID. He requested the Minister to continue his support and advise on the "Family Care First” project to continue contributing to the diversion and closure of Residential Care Institutions as well as to prevent the separation of children from families, alternative care for family, and the permanent plan, and to provide the capacity building for social services workforce. He also asked the Minister to increase the national budget to accelerate the social assistance mechanism that places children at heart and supports resources to the social service workforce.

In response, H.E. Vong Sauth expressed his appreciation and highly valued Save the Children's cooperation and past work. He also responded positively with full support towards the suggestions raised and encouraged the senior leaders of Save the Children to continue the dialogue with the specialized units on the detailed technical work at the ministry level. In addition, the Minister highlighted the efforts made by the government in the field of social protection, especially the transfer of cash to families with ID Poor, families affected by Covid-19 and families affected by the floods, totaling of more than one million families.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Courtesy Meeting With H.E Vong Sauth[:]

[:en]Phnom Penh: On February 28, 2023, the 21st Family Care First Learning Summit was organized at the Himawari Hotel. There were 96 participants (40 women), including representatives of the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation, Ministry of Interior, Ministry of Information, Ministry of Labour, Ministry of Education, Youth, and Sport, the Department of Social Affairs, the WCCC, NGO Partners, Development Partners, and Independent Researchers. post.post_content:
[:en] [Phnom Penh] Morning of February 20, 2023,Family Care First (FCF) of Save the Children in collaboration with National Institute of Social Affairs (NISA) of Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation to organize Training of Trainers for the Social Service Workforce Curriculum Level 1 from February 20-23, 2023, with a total of 30 participants (15 women). This training supported by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) and Save the Children. The purpose of this training is to cooperate with the Ministry of Social Affairs in training national trainers to help social services focus on child protection and to enable national trainers to provide adequate training at the sub-national level on a number of common modules:
  • Concepts and social support
  • Ecosystem model theory
  • Communication skills

post.post_content:
[:en]On January 26, 2023, The Knowledge Sharing Group (KSG) met to support FCF implement its Knowledge Sharing Strategy, which is based on the four stages of the Knowledge Management Cycle (Knowledge Generation, Knowledge Capture, Knowledge Sharing, and Knowledge Application) as outlined by the USAID Knowledge Management Framework.

There were 10 participants (4 women) of the KSG members including representatives from M’lup Russey, Cambodian Children Trust (CCT), Friends International (FI), This Life Cambodia (TLC), and UNICEF discussed some outstanding agenda from the last meeting including finalizing the Term of Reference and creating consensus on the list of Researches/Studies to be included in the Meta Review of Family-Based Care in Cambodia, updating on the Learning Exchange Visit that will be on February 09, 2023, and presenting the partner survey/ KII’s key findings and recommendations.[:]

This support has enabled the children to remain safe, and continue with their education. [:en]
On 24 November, Save the Children through FCF | REACT, in collaboration with national and sub-national government, held a handover ceremony for migrant children and families returning to Cambodia. post.post_content:
[:en]កាលពីម្សិលមិញ យើងបានរៀបចំដំណើរទស្សនៈកិច្ចជូន លោកស្រី នែនស៊ី ជែ អ៊ីសលីក នាយិកាបេសកកម្ម និងបុគ្គលិកផ្សេងទៀត របស់ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID Cambodia ដើម្បីទៅពិនិត្រមើលគម្រោង FCF | REACT ដែលអនុវត្តដោយអង្គការទំនុកចិត្តកុមារ។ យើងបានជួបជាមួយ កញ្ញា ស៊ីណេត ដែលពីមុនធ្លាប់រស់នៅក្នុងមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារ ហើយមានចិត្តចង់ធានាថា កុមារដទៃទៀតមិនត្រូវបានដាក់នៅក្នុងមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារទៀតឡើយ។ ប្អូនកំពុងដឹកនាំក្រុមថ្មីដ៏សកម្មមួយ ដែលផ្តល់ការគាំទ្រ និងការអភិវឌ្ឍជំនាញដល់កុមារ និងយុវជនដែលបានចាកចេញពីមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារ។ យើងក៏បានជួបជាមួយគ្រួសារមួយទៀតដែលទទួលបានការគាំទ្រក្នុងការចាប់ផ្តើមកសិដ្ឋានដាំផ្សិត ដើម្បីជួយស្តារស្ថានភាពសេដ្ឋកិច្ចក្រោយពីការខាតបង់ប្រាក់ចំណូលកំឡុងពេលជំងឺកូវីដ១៩ និងជួយកូនៗរបស់ពួកគេឱ្យបន្តការសិក្សាទៅមុខទៀត។ សូមថ្លែងអំណរគុណដល់ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID និងរដ្ឋាភិបាលកម្ពុជាសម្រាប់ការបន្តគាំទ្រដល់ការងារដ៏សំខាន់នេះ!

[:kh]កាលពីម្សិលមិញ យើងបានរៀបចំដំណើរទស្សនៈកិច្ចជូន លោកស្រី នែនស៊ី ជែ អ៊ីសលីក នាយិកាបេសកកម្ម និងបុគ្គលិកផ្សេងទៀត របស់ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID Cambodia ដើម្បីទៅពិនិត្រមើលគម្រោង FCF | REACT ដែលអនុវត្តដោយអង្គការទំនុកចិត្តកុមារ។ យើងបានជួបជាមួយ កញ្ញា ស៊ីណេត ដែលពីមុនធ្លាប់រស់នៅក្នុងមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារ ហើយមានចិត្តចង់ធានាថា កុមារដទៃទៀតមិនត្រូវបានដាក់នៅក្នុងមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារទៀតឡើយ។ ប្អូនកំពុងដឹកនាំក្រុមថ្មីដ៏សកម្មមួយ ដែលផ្តល់ការគាំទ្រ និងការអភិវឌ្ឍជំនាញដល់កុមារ និងយុវជនដែលបានចាកចេញពីមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារ។ យើងក៏បានជួបជាមួយគ្រួសារមួយទៀតដែលទទួលបានការគាំទ្រក្នុងការចាប់ផ្តើមកសិដ្ឋានដាំផ្សិត ដើម្បីជួយស្តារស្ថានភាពសេដ្ឋកិច្ចក្រោយពីការខាតបង់ប្រាក់ចំណូលកំឡុងពេលជំងឺកូវីដ១៩ និងជួយកូនៗរបស់ពួកគេឱ្យបន្តការសិក្សាទៅមុខទៀត។ សូមថ្លែងអំណរគុណដល់ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID និងរដ្ឋាភិបាលកម្ពុជាសម្រាប់ការបន្តគាំទ្រដល់ការងារដ៏សំខាន់នេះ!

post.post_content:
[:en]Yesterday we hosted USAID Head of Mission, Nancy J. Eslick, and a number of USAID Cambodia staff to visit the FCF | REACT projects implemented by Cambodian Children's Trust. We met with Sinet who previously lived in an orphanage, and is passionate about ensuring other children are not placed in orphanages. She is leading an exciting new group that provides peer support and skill development to children and young people who have left orphanages. We also met with a household who has received support to start a mushroom farm, to help recover from income losses due to COVID, and enable their child to continue with their education. Thanks to USAID and the Cambodian government for their continued support to enable this important work!

The COVID-19 in our country is still a concern and recently getting worse. Responding to this situation, FCF | REACT of Save the Children Cambodia managed to distribute the 2nd batch during 2021 of Personal Protective Equipment and Risk Communication and Community Engagement materials to our 16 partners and member organizations. post.post_content:
[:en]
Sila, 13 years old, was a former child placed in a residential care institution together with his 4 siblings. After family reunification, the family’s condition was still limited until his mother passed away due to flooding last year, ending up them to live in a plastic-tarp shelter on the street with their grandparents.
With the support of European Union in Cambodia and USAID Cambodia through FCF | REACT, Cambodian Children's Trust staff immediately assessed the family conditions and began to support them in providing food, water, blankets, pillows, tents and medical care. Moreover, the new safe house was also built to replace the damaged house with the shared support from 2 other NGOs.
Throughout the pandemic, CCT staff assists Sila in getting ready to return to school, offers medical care to his grandparents, and stays with the family every step of the way to ensure they have what they need. With all this support, the grandparents are quite happy to live in a new warm house with all their grandchildren.
[:kh]
សិលា អាយុ១៣ឆ្នាំ ធ្លាប់រស់នៅក្នុងមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមាររួមជាមួយបងប្អូនបង្កើតរបស់ប្អូន៤រូបទៀត។ បន្ទាប់ពីបានបង្រួបបង្រួមទៅកាន់គ្រួសារ ស្ថានភាពគ្រួសារនៅតែមានកម្រិត រហូតដល់ពេលម្តាយរបស់ប្អូនបានបាត់បង់ជីវិតក្នុងគ្រោះទឹកជំនន់កាលពីឆ្នាំមុន ដែលធ្វើឱ្យផ្ទះរបស់ប្អូនដែលត្រូវរស់នៅជាមួយតាយាយត្រូវខូចខាត និងត្រូវស្នាក់នៅក្នុងតង់នៅលើផ្លូវមួយរយៈ។
នៅក្នុងពេលនោះ ជាមួយការគាំទ្ររបស់សហភាពអឺរ៉ុប និងទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយសហរដ្ឋអាមេរិក (USAID) តាមរយៈគម្រោង FCF | REACT អង្គការទំនុកចិត្តកុមារកម្ពុជា បានប៉ាន់ប្រមាណតម្រូវការគ្រួសាររបស់សិលា និងផ្តល់ការគាំទ្រពួកគាត់ជាមួយនឹងអាហារ ទឹកស្អាត ភួយ ខ្នើយ តង់ និងការថែទាំសុខភាពជាប្រចាំ។ លើសពីនេះ ផ្ទះថ្មីមួយដែលមានសុវត្ថិភាព ត្រូវបានសាងសង់ជូនពួកគាត់ដែលជាការចូលរួមចំណែកជាមួយអង្គការពីរផ្សេងទៀត។
ក្នុងអំឡុងពេលនៃការឆ្លងរីករាលដាលជំំងឺកូវីដ១៩នេះ អង្គការទំនុកចិត្តកុមារកម្ពុជា បាននិងកំពុងគាំទ្រសិលាក្នុងការត្រៀមខ្លួនសម្រាប់ការចូលរៀនឡើងវិញនៅពេលសាលាបើកដំណើរការជាថ្មី ផ្តល់ការថែទាំសុខភាពដល់លោកយាយលោកតា និងបន្តតាមដានពួកគាត់ដើម្បីអាចដឹងពីតម្រូវការផ្សេងៗទៀត។ ជាមួយនឹងការគាំទ្រ និងកិច្ចខិតខំប្រឹងប្រែងទាំងអស់នេះ លោកយាយលោកតា ពិតជាសប្បាយរីករាយណាស់ និងមានភាពកក់ក្តៅក្នុងការរស់នៅជាមួយចៅៗក្នុងផ្ទះដ៏កក់ក្តៅ។
[:]
post.post_title: [:en]A warm house![:kh]ផ្ទះដ៏កក់ក្តៅ![:]

post.post_content:
[:en]
Mrs. San Van, a single mother, is taking care of 2 children (a 12 year-old daughter and 3-year-old son) in Battambang. Like other Cambodian families, the Covid-19 pandemic has reduced her income from selling products in the village. At the end of 2020, Van’s family was selected to receive cash transfers from FCF | REACT funded by USAID Cambodia through Komar Rikreay Association - KMR to support the children’s basic needs such as food and health care.
During these 6 months, she manages to save some money to raise some chickens and ducks and continue growing crops so that she can gets prepared when the cash assistance ends in July 2021.Currently, she owns 10 ducks and 20 chickens in her house and hopes to increase the amount in the next few months.
[:kh]
បងស្រី សាន វ៉ាន់ ជាម្តាយនៃកូនៗចំនួន២រូប (កូនស្រីអាយុ១២ឆ្នាំ និងកូនប្រុសអាយុ៣ឆ្នាំ) នៅខេត្តបាត់ដំបង។ ដូចគ្រួសារខ្មែរដទៃទៀតដែរ ការឆ្លងរីករាលដាលនៃជំងឺកូវីដ១៩ បានកាត់បន្ថយប្រាក់ចំណូលរបស់គាត់ពីការលក់ដូរនៅក្នុងភូមិ។ នៅចុងឆ្នាំ២០២០ ក្រុមគ្រួសាររបស់បងស្រីវ៉ាន់ ត្រូវបានជ្រើសរើសឱ្យទទួលជំនួយសាច់ប្រាក់ពីគម្រោង FCF | REACT ដែលគាំទ្រដោយទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយសហរដ្ឋអាមេរិក តាមរយៈអង្គការកុមាររីករាយ ដើម្បីគាំទ្រដល់តម្រូវការមូលដ្ឋានរបស់កូនៗ ដូចជាអាហារ និងការថែទាំសុខភាព។
ក្នុងអំឡុងពេល៦ខែនេះ បងស្រី បានគ្រប់គ្រង និងសន្សំប្រាក់មួួយផ្នែកដើម្បីចិញ្ចឹមមាន់ទាខ្លះ និងបន្តដាំដំណាំបន្តទៀត ដើម្បីឱ្យគ្រួសារបងស្រីអាចត្រៀមខ្លួននៅពេលដែលកម្មវិធីជំនួយជាសាច់ប្រាក់បញ្ចប់នៅខែកក្កដា ឆ្នាំ២០២១នេះ។ នៅពេលនេះ បងស្រី មានទា១០ក្បាល និងមាន់២០ក្បាលនៅក្នុងផ្ទះ ហើយសង្ឃឹមថានឹងកើនឡើងនៅក្នុងខែបន្តបន្ទាប់។
[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Using Cash Transfer for Small Business[:kh]ការប្រើប្រាស់សាច់ប្រាក់ឧបត្ថម្ភសម្រាប់របរខ្នាតតូច[:]

post.post_content: [:en]The cash transfers have been in progress since December 2020 to ensure that children can stay safe and healthy in their families during COVID-19. Still, as part of USAID Cambodia support, more than 48 households that are receiving case management by the MoSVY were added to have received the monthly cash support since April this year. In total, 750 households will receive this support until July. Thanks to all the implementing partners who are joining hands to support all the families.[:kh]ការឧបត្ថម្ភសាច់ប្រាក់ បាននិងកំពុងដំណើរចាប់តាំងពីខែធ្នូ ឆ្នាំ២០២០ ដើម្បីធានាថាកុមារដែលយើងកំពុងគាំទ្រ អាចរស់នៅក្នុងការថែទាំក្នុងគ្រួសារប្រកបដោយសុវត្ថិភាព និងភាពលូតលាស់ក្នុងអំឡុងពេលនៃជំងឺកូវីដ១៩នេះ។ នៅតែជាផ្នែកមួយនៃការគំាទ្ររបស់ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយសហរដ្ឋអាមេរិកតាមរយៈ FCF | REACT គ្រួសារបន្ថែមចំនួន៤៨ ដែលកំពុងទទួលការគ្រប់គ្រងករណីដោយក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា បានចាប់ផ្តើមទទួលការឧបត្ថម្ភសាច់ប្រាក់ប្រចាំខែចាប់តាំងពីខែមេសា ឆ្នាំ២០២១នេះ។ ជាសរុប គ្រួសារទទួលផលពីកម្មវិធីជំនួយកុមារតាមរយៈការឧបត្ថម្ភសាច់ប្រាក់ មានចំនួន៧៥០ ដែលទទួលជំនួយសាច់ប្រាក់រហូតដល់ខែកក្កដា។ សូមអរគុណដល់អង្គការដៃគូទាំងអស់ដែលបានសហការយ៉ាងល្អក្នុងការដំណើរកម្មវិធី និងគាំទ្រគ្រួសារទទួលផលទាំងអស់។[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Households to Receive Cash Transfers[:kh]Households to Receive Cash Transfers[:]

post.post_content: [:en]During COVID-19, some children might need to go working due to the school closure, family income reduction and lack of food. Besides our existing child protection work, Cash Transfer is shown through the post-distribution survey to generate many positive effects on households, one of which is that the child will not have to go to work.[:kh]ក្នុងអំឡុងពេល នៃជំងឺកូវីដ១៩នេះ កុមារមួយចំនួន ត្រូវចេញទៅធ្វើការខាងក្រៅដោយសារមូលហេតុនៃការបិទសាលា ការធ្លាក់ចុះនូវប្រាក់ចំណូលគ្រួសារ និងកង្វះខាតអាហារ។ ក្រៅពីការគាំទ្រជាប្រចាំរបស់គម្រោង FCF | REACT ក្នុងកិច្ចការពារកុមារ កម្មវិធីជំនួយកុមារតាមរយៈការឧបត្ថម្ភសាច់ប្រាក់ បានផ្តល់នូវផលជះវិជ្ជមានបន្ថែមចំពោះក្រុមគ្រួសារដែលយើងកំពុងគាំទ្រ។ តាមរយៈការស្ទង់មតិរបស់កម្មវិធី ផលជះចម្បងមួយក្នុងចំណោមផលជះទាំងអស់ គឺកុមារ មិនចាំបាច់ចេញទៅធ្វើការ។[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Positive Effects of Child-sensitive Cash Assistance on Households[:kh]ផលជះវិជ្ជមានបន្ថែមចំពោះក្រុមគ្រួសារតាមរយៈកម្មវិធីឧបត្ថម្ភសាច់ប្រាក់[:]

post.post_content: [:en]FCF|REACT is very pleased to support many children across Cambodia to ensure that their children can remain in a safe and sustainable family. We want to take this opportunity to promote love and caring among family members, especially during this challenging time to ensure the wellbeing of everyone![:kh]FCF|REACT ពិតជាមានភាពរីករាយជាពន់ពេកក្នុងការគាំទ្រកុមារជាច្រើននៅកម្ពុជា ដើម្បីជួយដល់ពួកគេឱ្យបានរស់នៅក្នុងការថែទាំក្នុងគ្រួសារ ប្រកបដោយសុវត្ថិភាព ភាពលូតលាស់ និងនិរន្តរភាព។ ឆ្លៀតក្នុងឪកាសនេះ យើងសូមលើកកម្ពស់ការផ្តល់ក្តីនូវស្រលាញ់ និងការយកចិត្តទុកដាក់រវាងសមាជិកក្នុងគ្រួសារ ជាពិសេសនៅក្នុងអំឡុងពេលដ៏លំបាកនេះដើម្បីធានានូវសុខុមាលភាពរបស់អ្នករាល់គ្នានៅជុំវិញខ្លួន។[:]

With the recent increases in community transmission of COVID-19 throughout Cambodia and school closures happening in different provinces, some children are once again away from their friends and activities that they enjoy. Here is a short video to learn about 5 essential tips to improve children's mental health through these challenging times! Please share this video with anyone who you think might help. Stay safe, everyone![:kh][embed]https://youtu.be/HFSwwBuq5nY[/embed]

ស្របពេលដែលកើតមានឡើងនូវការឆ្លងរីករាលដាលនៃជំងឺកូវីដ-១៩ក្នុងសហគមន៍ ហើយសាលារៀន ត្រូវបានបិទទ្វារសារជាថ្មីនៅតាមបណ្តារាជធានីខេត្តផ្សេងៗនៅទូទាំងប្រទេសកម្ពុជា កុមារភាគច្រើនត្រូវនៅឃ្លាតឆ្ងាយពីមិត្តភក្តិម្តងទៀត និងមិនអាចរីករាយជាមួយសកម្មភាពដែលពួកគេធ្លាប់មាន។ នេះគឺជាវីដេអូខ្លីមួយដែលអប់រំអំពីគន្លឹះសំខាន់ៗចំនួនប្រាំ ដើម្បីលើកកម្ពស់សុខុមាលភាពផ្លូវចិត្តរបស់កុមារ នៅក្នុងពេលវេលាដ៏លំបាកមួយនេះ! សូមទស្សនា និងចែករំលែកបន្តទៅកាន់អ្នកដែលអ្នកគិតថា ពួកគេកំពុងត្រូវការចំណេះដឹងទាំងនេះ។ សូមរក្សាសុវត្ថិភាពរបស់ខ្លួនឱ្យបានខ្ជាប់ខ្ជួនគ្រប់ពេលវេលា![:]

អង្គការ Save the Children រួមជាមួយអង្គការដៃគូចំនួន៧ កំពុងជួយលើកកម្ពស់សមត្ថភាពរបស់គ្រួសារទទួលផលក្នុងការថែទាំកូនៗរបស់ពួកគេ ដើម្បីសម្រេចឱ្យបាននូវសមទ្ធិផលនៃកិច្ចការពារកុមារប្រកបដោយភាពវិជ្ជមាន។ ដោយមានការគាំទ្រពីទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយសហរដ្ឋអាមេរិកដើម្បីការអភិវឌ្ឃអន្តរជាតិ (USAID) និងសហភាពអឺរ៉ុប គម្រោង FCF|REACT កំពុងផ្តល់នូវការឧបត្ថម្ភសាច់ប្រាក់ប្រចាំខែ និងផ្តល់ការគាំទ្រដល់អ្នកផ្តល់ការថែទាំក្នុងការចាត់អាទិភាពនូវការចំណាយរបស់ពួកគេដើម្បីឧត្តមប្រយោជន៍របស់កូនៗ។[:]

ស្របពេល​ដែលកើតមានឡើងនូវការរីករាលដាលនៃជំងឺកូវីដ-១៩ក្នុងសហគមន៍នៅទូទាំងប្រទេសកម្ពុជា យើងសូមចែករំលែកនូវវីដេអូមួយ ស្តីពីការគាំទ្រដល់បុគ្គលិកកាន់ករណី ក្នុងការធានាថា កុមារ និងគ្រួសារពួកគេ ដែលកំពុងទទួលការគាំទ្រពីយើង រួមទាំងខ្លួនបុគ្គលិកផ្ទាល់មានសុវត្ថិភាព។ យើងសង្ឃឹមថាវីដេអូខ្លីនេះ នឹងផ្តល់ជាសារប្រយោជន៍ដល់អ្នក និងក្រុមការងារ។ សូមថ្លែងអំណរគុណដល់ក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា សម្រាប់ការដឹកនាំការផលិត ដើម្បីឱ្យវីដេអូនេះសមស្របជាមួយគោលការណ៍ណែនាំ ស្តីពីការគ្រប់គ្រងករណីក្នុងអំឡុងពេលជំងឺកូវីដ១៩។ សូមរក្សាសុវត្ថិភាពរបស់ខ្លួនឱ្យបានខ្ជាប់ខ្ជួនគ្រប់ពេលវេលា។[:]

post.post_content: [:en] It is recommended by the Ministry of Health that in addition to wearing mask and frequently washing our hands, we should keep a physical distance of 1.5 meters from other people​ to protect oursleves during COVID-19. Enjoy watching and share it to your beloved people.[:kh] តាមការណែនាំរបស់ក្រសួងសុខាភិបាល យើងត្រូវរក្សាគម្លាតសុវត្ថិភាពបុគ្គលយ៉ាងហោចណាស់ ១,៥ ម៉ែត្រពីគ្នា បន្ថែមពីការពាក់ម៉ាស់ និងការលាងដៃឱ្យបានញឹកញាប់ដើម្បីការពារខ្លួនពីការឆ្លងជំងឺកូវីដ១៩។ តើអ្នកដឹងទេថា ហេតុអ្វីបានជាគម្លាតសុវត្ថិភាពបុគ្គលមានសារៈសំខាន់សម្រាប់ការពារខ្លួន? សូមទស្សនាគំនូរជីវចលស្តីពីគម្លាតសុវត្ថិភាពបុគ្គល និងឈ្វេងយល់ពីសារៈសំខាន់នៃការអនុវត្តគម្លាតសុវត្ថិភាពបុគ្គលនេះ។[:]

[:en]On 26th October, 2020, Family Care First | REACT convened our Learning Summit (online and in person) presided over by H.E. Nim Thoth, Standing Secretary of State, the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation (MoSVY). The 63 participants had the opportunity to receive updates and share knowledge to increase the percentage of children living in safe, nurturing family-based care. We would like to thank MoSVY and participants who engaged, including donors European Union in Cambodian and USAID Cambodia.

 [:kh]នៅថ្ងៃទី២៦ ខែតុលា ឆ្នាំ២០២០ គម្រោងFamily Care First | REACT បានរៀបចំកិច្ចប្រជុំដៃគូដោយផ្ទាល់ និងតាមអនឡាញ ក្រោមអធិបតីភាពរបស់ ឯកឧត្តម នឹម ថូត រដ្ឋលេខាធិការប្រចាំការនៃ ក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា។ អ្នកចូលរួមទាំង៦៣រូប មានឱកាសទទួលបាន​ និងចែករំលែកព័ត៌មានអំពីបច្ចុប្បន្នភាពនៃការកើនឡើងភាគរយនៃកុមារដែលរស់នៅក្នុងការថែទាំនៅក្នុងគ្រួសារ ប្រកបដោយសុវត្ថិភាព និងភាពលូតលាស់។ យើងសូមថ្លែងអំណរគុណចំពោះក្រសួងស.អ.យ និងតំណាងសមាជិកអង្គការដៃគូទាំងអស់ដែលចូលរួមក្នុងកិច្ចប្រជុំនេះ ព្រមទាំងស្ថាប័នម្ចាស់ជំនួយដែលរួមមាន សហភាពអឺរ៉ុប និងទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយសហរដ្ឋអាមេរិក USAID។

post.post_content: [:en]On the 22nd of July 2020, H.E Touch Channy, the Director General of the Directorate of Technical Affaires of the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation, chaired the official launching of Primero at Sunway Hotel. Primero is the Child Protection Digital Case Management System, which will be used nationwide in Cambodia. View more photos. (Content and photo from the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation)[:kh]ភ្នំពេញ៖ ថ្ងៃទី២២ ខែកក្កដា ឆ្នាំ២០២០ ឯកឧត្តម តូច ចាន់នី អគ្គនាយកនៃអគ្គនាយកដ្ឋានបច្ចេកទេស ក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា អញ្ជើញជាអធិបតី ក្នុងពិធីផ្សព្វផ្សាយប្រកាសដាក់ឱ្យប្រើប្រាស់ជាផ្លូវការ នៃប្រព័ន្ធបច្ចេកវិទ្យា Primero សម្រាប់ការគ្រប់គ្រងករណីកិច្ចការពារកុមារនៅកម្ពុជា កម្មវិធីនេះធ្វើឡើងនៅសណ្ឋាគារសាន់វ៉េភ្នំពេញ។ មើលរូបភាពបន្ថែម (រូបភាព និងអត្ថបទដោយក្រសួងស.អ.យ)[:]

post.post_content: [:en] Do not discriminate the COVID-19 positive people. We hate COVID-19, but we should not hate the COVID-19 positive people. We can protect ourselves by washing our hands with soap and water. We can also use an alcohol-based hand sanitizer, and do not touch our mouth, eyes and nose. This animation is supported by USAID Cambodia Breakthrough ACTION + RESEARCH[:kh] ជំងឺកូវីដ១៩ គួរឱ្យស្អប់ ប៉ុន្តែយើងមិនត្រូវស្អប់អ្នកឆ្លងមេរោគកូវីដ១៩ទេ ហើយយើងអាចការពារខ្លួនបានដោយអនុវត្តវិធីងាយៗ ដូចជាលាងដៃជាមួយសាប៊ូ អាកុល ឬជែល មិនត្រូវយកដៃប៉ះមាត់ ភ្នែក និងច្រមុះ ជាដើម។ គំនូរជីវចលនេះត្រូវបានគាំទ្រដោយ USAID Cambodia Breakthrough ACTION + RESEARCH[:]

post.post_content: [:en] Sarath was placed in a residential care institution when she was 13. With support from the European Union in Cambodia, USAID Cambodia and Save the Children Hong Kong 香港救助兒童會, through Family Care First | REACT, Sarath was supported to move out of a residential care institution into independent living once she was a young adult. She is now a university student with scholarship support from Children's Future International and is working part-time to support herself.[:kh] សារ៉ាត់ត្រូវបានយកទៅទុកដាក់នៅមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារនៅពេលដែលនាងមានអាយុ១៣ឆ្នាំ។ ដោយមានការគាំទ្រពីសហភាពអឺរ៉ុប ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID និងអង្គការ Save the Children ហុងកុង តាមរយៈគម្រោងថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារជាអាទិភាពចម្បង និង REACT សារ៉ាត់ត្រូវបានគាំទ្រឱ្យចាកចេញពីមណ្ឌលទៅរស់នៅដោយឯករាជ្យនៅពេលដែលនាងពេញវ័យ។ នៅពេលនេះ នាងកំពុងតែរៀននៅមហាវិទ្យាល័យ ដោយទទួលបានអាហារូបករណ៍ពី អង្គការអនាគតកុមារអន្តរជាតិ ហើយធ្វើការក្រៅម៉ោងដើម្បីចិញ្ចឹមខ្លួនឯង។  [:]

post.post_content: [:en]With support from USAID Cambodia, we have delivered hygiene materials to the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation and 19 NGOs in our network.[:kh]ដោយមានការគាំទ្រពីទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID យើងបានចែកចាយសម្ភារៈអនាម័យទៅកាន់ ក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា និងអង្គភាពក្នុងបណ្តាញចំនួន១៩ផ្សេងទៀត។[:]

post.post_content: [:en]Srey Oun* who was born with a brain abnormality needs regular physiotherapy to improve her mobility. With essential hygiene materials provided by USAID Cambodia, Dany, a Community Rehabilitation Team member from Children In Families, is able to provide regular physiotherapy sessions, ensuring appropriate precautions, during this #COVID19 pandemic. *Name has been changed for confidentiality.[:kh]ស្រីអូន* ដែលកើតមកជាមួយនឹងពិការភាពខួរក្បាលត្រូវការការព្យាបាលដោយចលនាជាប្រចាំដើម្បីអភិវឌ្ឍការធ្វើចលនារបស់នាង។ ដោយមានសម្ភារៈអនាម័យគ្រប់គ្រាន់ពី ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID ដានី ដែលជាក្រុមការងារផ្នែកការស្តារនីតិសម្បទាសហគមន៍ របស់អង្គការកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារ អាចផ្តល់នូវការព្យាបាលចលនាជាប្រចាំ ខណៈដែលធានានូវការការពារ ក្នុងអំឡុងពេលរីករាលដាលនៃ #ជំងឺកូវីដ១៩ នេះ។ *ឈ្មោះត្រូវបានផ្លាស់ប្តូរដើម្បីការពារអត្តសញ្ញាណកុមារ។[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Providing Physiotherapy During COVID-19[:kh]ការព្យាបាលដោយចលនា ក្នុងអំឡុងពេលរាករាលដាលនៃជំងឺកូវីដ១៩[:]

post.post_content: [:en]Family Care First | REACT, UNICEF Cambodia, Friends-International and Mith Samlanh participated in meetings with Kandal and Phnom Penh Department of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation. Working in close collaboration with government and other key stakeholders ensures the best possible outcomes for vulnerable children and their families. Thank you for this great collaboration.[:kh]ក្រុមការងាររបស់គម្រោងថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារជាអាទិភាពចម្បង និង REACT អង្គការយូនីសេហ្វ អង្គការហ្វ្រេនអន្តរជាតិ និងមិត្តសំឡាញ់ បានចូលរួមប្រជុំជាមួយនឹង មន្ទីរសង្គមកិច្ចអតីតយុទ្ធជននិងយុវនីតិសម្បទារាជធានីភ្នំពេញ និងខេត្តកណ្តាល។ ការសហការយ៉ាងជិតស្និទ្ធជាមួយរដ្ឋាភិបាល និងដៃគូពាក់ព័ន្ធ អាចធានាបាននូវលទ្ធផលដែលល្អបំផុតសម្រាប់កុមារ និងគ្រួសារ។ យើងខ្ញុំសូមអរគុណចំពោះកិច្ចសហការកន្លងមក។[:]

post.post_content: [:en] Many initiatives have been created during this period by Family Care First | REACT funded partners to ensure that the clients that we work with continue to receive services and support, while being protected from #COVID19. Together with the support from European Union in Cambodiaand USAID Cambodia, we help vulnerable children and families at a time when they need us the most.[:kh] គំនិតច្នៃប្រឌិតជាច្រើនត្រូវបានបង្កើតឡើងដោយសមាជិកគម្រោងថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារ និង REACT ក្នុងអំឡុងពេលនេះ ដើម្បីធានាថាអតិថិជនដែលយើងធ្វើការជាមួយ នៅតែបន្តទទួលបានសេវាកម្ម និងការគាំទ្រ ខណៈដែលទទួលបានការការពារពីជំងឺ #កូវីដ១៩។ ដោយធ្វើការសហការគ្នា ក្រោមការគាំទ្រពីសហភាពអឺរ៉ុប និងទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID យើងជួយដល់កុមារ និងគ្រួសារដែលងាយរងគ្រោះ នៅពេលដែលពួកគាត់ត្រូវការយើងបំផុត។[:]

post.post_content: [:en]Today we were honoured to host US Ambassador W. Patrick Murphy at Save the Children. We provided him with updates on how we're responding to COVID-19 in Cambodia, to ensure that vulnerable children and their families are safe, healthy and protected. The Ambassador presented our partner, Children In Families, with essential hygiene materials to enable their social workers to undertake safe visits to the children and families they support. With USAID Cambodia funding, Family Care First - REACT 's Save the Children is able to provide this support to 19 NGO's within our network.[:kh]នៅថ្ងៃនេះ អង្គការ Save the Children បានទទួលស្វាគមន៍លោកឯកអគ្គរដ្ឋទូត ប្រចាំព្រះរាជាណាចក្រកម្ពុជា W. Patrick Murphy នៅឯការិយាល័យរបស់អង្គការ។ យើងបានជម្រាបប្រាប់ដល់លោកឯកអគ្គរដ្ឋទូតពីបច្ចុប្បន្នភាពស្តីពីការឆ្លើយតបទៅនឹងការរាលដាលនៃជំងឺកូវីដ១៩នៅក្នុងប្រទេសកម្ពុជា ដើម្បីធានាថាកុមារដែលជួបការលំបាកនិងគ្រួសាររបស់ពួកគេមានសុវត្ថិភាព សុខភាពល្អ និងទទួលបានការការពារ។ លោកឯកអគ្គរដ្ឋទូតបានផ្តល់ដល់ដៃគូ កុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារ នូវសម្ភារៈអនាម័យដែលជាតម្រូវការចាំបាច់ ដើម្បីឱ្យបុគ្គលិកសង្គមក្នុងការជួយដល់កុមារនិងក្រុមគ្រួសារ ដែលពួកគេគាំទ្រ។ ដោយមានជំនួយមូលនិធិពីទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក ដោយមានជំនួយមូលនិធិពីទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិករបស់អង្គការ Save the Children បានផ្តល់សម្ភារៈអនាម័យទាំងនោះដល់១៩ដៃគូនៅក្នុងបណ្តាញរបស់យើង។[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]If you are between 13 – 24 year old, create a 2-minute video on the topic "No Place Like Home" for the chance to win Canon EOS M50 Mirrorless Camera. This video can be a story or a documentary that show the value, love, warmth and the child development in family based care. Please submit the video to fcf-react@savethechildren.org by 30 November 2019.

Terms and Conditions

Entry must be produced and submitted by youth aged 13 – 24 year old. The video must not be longer than 2 minutes. The entry must be your original work. People featuring in the video must provide their consent and understand that the video will be available for public viewing. The written consent form have to be submitted with the video entry. Family Care First | REACT and our donors reserve the right to repost the video on our communication platforms. Family members of Save the Children’s staff cannot participate in this competition.

Evaluating Process

30% of the score will be given based on the number of social media engagement. (1 view = 1 point; 1 reaction = 1 point; 1 comment = 1 point; 1 share = 2 points) 70% of the score will be given by the video committee. The committee is led by Mr. Chhour Sopannha, Director of Social Welfare Department, the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation and other Family Care First | REACT members. Points will be given for originality, key messages, creativity and audio/visual quality.[:kh]
ប្រសិនបើប្អូនមានអាយុពី១៣ដល់២៤ឆ្នាំ ប្អូនអាចផលិតវីដេអូខ្លីរយៈពេល២នាទី អំពី “គ្មានទីណាប្រសើរជាងគ្រួសារ” ដើម្បីឈ្នះរង្វាន់ Canon EOS M50 Mirrorless Camera។ វីដេអូនេះអាចជាសាច់រឿងសម្តែង ឬសាច់រឿងជាក់ស្តែង ដែលបង្ហាញពីគុណតម្លៃរបស់គ្រួសារ សេចក្តីស្រឡាញ់ ភាពកក់ក្តៅ និងការអភិវឌ្ឍដែលកុមារអាចទទួលបាននៅក្នុងគ្រួសារ។ ពិន្ទុនឹងផ្តលជូនចំពោះ ខ្លឹមសារនៃវីដេអូ ភាពច្នៃប្រឌិត និងគុណភាពនៃការផលិត។
 
សូមផ្ញើវីដេអូនោះមកកាន់ fcf-react@savethechildren.org មុនថ្ងៃទី៣០ ខែវិច្ឆិកា ឆ្នាំ២០១៩។

លក្ខខណ្ឌនៃការចូលរួម

  • អ្នកចូលរួមត្រូវតែមានអាយុពី១៣ ដល់២៤ឆ្នាំ
  • វីដេអូនេះត្រូវតែមិនវែងជាង២នាទី
  • វីដេអូនេះជាស្នាដៃរបស់អ្នកចូលរួម
  • អ្នកដែលចូលរួមក្នុងវីដេអូនេះត្រូវតែផ្តល់ការអនុវញ្ញាតិដល់បេក្ខជន និងយល់ថាវីដេអូនេះនឹងត្រូវផ្សព្វផ្សាយជាសាធារណៈ។ សូមផ្ញើលិខិតអនុវញ្ញាតិមកកាន់យើងខ្ញុំពេលដែលអ្នកផ្ញើវីដេអូមក។
  • Family Care First | REACT និងម្ចាស់ជំនួយរបស់យើង រក្សាសិទ្ធិក្នុងការផ្សព្វផ្សាយវីដេអូរបស់អ្នកតាមប្រព័ន្ធផ្សព្វផ្សាយរបស់យើង
  • ក្រុមគ្រួសាររបស់បុគ្គលិកអង្គការ Save the Children មិនត្រូវបានអនុញ្ញាតិឱ្យចូលរួមការប្រកួតនេះឡើយ

ការវាយតម្លៃ

  • ៣០% នៃពិន្ទុសរុបនឹងផ្តល់ជូនដល់ប្រជាប្រិយភាពតាមបណ្តាញសង្គម (1 view = 1 point; 1 reaction = 1 point; 1 comment = 1 point; 1 share = 2 points)
  • ៧០% នៃពិន្ទុនឹងផ្តល់ដោយគណៈកម្មាការវាយតម្លៃ។ ប្រធាននៃគណៈកម្មាការនេះដឹកនាំដោយ លោកឈួរ សុបញ្ញា ប្រធាននាយកដ្ឋានសុខុមាលភាពសង្គម នៃក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា និងសមាជិកគម្រោង Family Care First | REACT ដទៃទៀត។ ពន្ទុនឹងផ្តលជូនចំពោះ ភាពដើម ខ្លឹមសារនៃវីដេអូ ភាពច្នៃប្រឌិត និងគុណភាពនៃការផលិត។

post.post_content:
[:en]What does your family mean to you? In Cambodia, more than 26,000 children were placed in residential care facilities according to the 2017 mapping by MoSVY. Approximately 80% of them have one or both parents living. Many parents and caregivers do not know that residential care can have a devastating impact on children’s physical, social, emotional and cognitive development, which lead to lifelong negative implications. You can help by raising awareness of the importance of raising children in families instead of residential care institutions. So how can you join? If you are between 13 – 19 year old, create a 2-minute video showing ‘what your family means to you’ for a chance to be feature in SWSB + Manith’s upcoming music video and win one of the prizes below: First prize:           Canon EOS M50 Mirrorless Camera Kit w/EF-M15-45mm Second prize:     A videography training course Third prize:         A photography training course Submit your video to familycarefirst@savethechildren.org by the 28th of July. Please read the terms and conditions for more details.  

Terms and Conditions

  • Entry must be produced and submitted by youth aged 13 – 19 year old.
  • The video must not be longer than 2 minutes.
  • The entry must be your original work.
  • People featuring in the video must provide their consent and understand that the video will be available for public viewing. The written consent form have to be submitted with the video entry.
  • Family Care First | REACT and our donors reserve the right to repost the video on our communication platforms.
  • Family members of Save the Children’s staff cannot participate in this competition.
  • Evaluating process:
    • 40% of the score will be given based on the number of social media engagement. (1 view = 1 point; 1 reaction = 1 point; 1 comment = 1 point; 1 share = 2 points)
    • 60% of the score will be given by the video committee. The committee is led by Mr. Chhour Sopannha, Director of Social Welfare Department, the Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation and other Family Care First | REACT members. Points will be given for originality, key messages, creativity and audio/visual quality.
[:kh]
ការប្រកួតវីដេអូយុវជនអំពី #គ្រួសារខ្ញុំ
តើគ្រួសាររបស់អ្នកមានន័យយ៉ាងណាសម្រាប់អ្នក?
នៅប្រទេសកម្ពុជា យើងមានកុមារជាង២ម៉ឺន៦ពាន់នាក់ រស់នៅក្នុងកន្លែងថែទាំកុមារ នៅឆ្នាំ២០១៧ បើយោងតាមក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា។ តែតាមពិតទៅប្រមាណ៨០%នៃកុមារទាំងនោះមាន ឪពុកឬម្តាយ ឬទាំងឪពុកនិងម្តាយដែលនៅរស់នៅឡើយ។​ ឪពុកម្តាយ និងអ្នកមើលថែកុមារភាគច្រើនមិនដឹងទេថាមណ្ឌលកុមារកំព្រា ឬកន្លែងថែទាំកុមារ អាចប៉ះពាល់យ៉ាងខ្លាំងទៅដល់ការលូតលាស់ទាំងផ្នែករាងកាយ សង្គម ផ្លូវចិត្ត និងពុទ្ធិរបស់កុមារ ហើយវាអាចមានឥទ្ធិពលលើកុមារពេញមួយជីវិត។
 
អ្នកទាំងអស់គ្នាអាចចូលរួមជាមួយពួកយើងបានក្នុងការលើកកម្ពស់ការថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារ ជាជាងដាក់កុមារក្នុងមណ្ឌលកុមារកំព្រា។
តើអ្នកអាចចូលរួមបានដោយរបៀបណា?
 
ប្រសិនបើអ្នកមានអាយុពី១៣ ដល់១៩ឆ្នាំ អ្នកអាចផលិតវីដេអូខ្លីរយៈពេល២នាទី អំពី “តើគ្រួសាររបស់អ្នកមានន័យយ៉ាងណាសម្រាប់អ្នក?” សម្រាប់ឱកាសក្នុងការបង្ហាញវីដេអូរបស់អ្នកក្នុងវីដេអូបទចម្រៀងថ្មីរបស់ក្រុមតូច និងម៉ានិត។ អ្នកក៏អាចនឹងឈ្នះរង្វាន់ខាងក្រោមផងដែរ!
រង្វាន់ទី១៖ កាម៉េរ៉ា Canon EOS M50 Mirrorless Camera Kit w/EF-M15-45mm។
រង្វាន់ទី២៖ ការរៀនផលិតវីដេអូដោយមិនគិតថ្លៃ
រង្វាន់ទី៣៖ ការរៀនថតរូបដោយមិនគិតថ្លៃ!
 
សូមផ្ញើវីដេអូនោះមកកាន់ familycarefirst@savethechildren.org មុនថ្ងៃទី២៨ ខែកក្កដា។ សូមអានលក្ខខណ្ឌនៃការចូលរួមឱ្យបានច្បាស់មុននឹងចូលរួមប្រកួត៖

លក្ខខណ្ឌនៃការចូលរួម

  • អ្នកចូលរួមត្រូវតែមានអាយុពី១៣ ដល់១៩ឆ្នាំ
  • វីដេអូនេះត្រូវតែមិនវែងជាង២នាទី
  • វីដេអូនេះជាស្នាដៃរបស់អ្នកចូលរួម
  • អ្នកដែលចូលរួមក្នុងវីដេអូនេះត្រូវតែផ្តល់ការអនុវញ្ញាតិដល់បេក្ខជន និងយល់ថាវីដេអូនេះនឹងត្រូវផ្សព្វផ្សាយជាសាធារណៈ។ សូមផ្ញើលិខិតអនុវញ្ញាតិមកកាន់យើងខ្ញុំពេលដែលអ្នកផ្ញើវីដេអូមក។
  • Family Care First | REACT និងម្ចាស់ជំនួយរបស់យើង រក្សាសិទ្ធិក្នុងការផ្សព្វផ្សាយវីដេអូរបស់អ្នកតាមប្រព័ន្ធផ្សព្វផ្សាយរបស់យើង

[:en]

The Ministry of Social Affairs, Veterans and Youth Rehabilitation has signed two Memorandum of Understandings with Save the Children in Cambodia on the 22nd of February 2019. The First MoU on Family Care First and REACT (Responsive and Effective Child Welfare Systems Transformation) projects funded by USAID Cambodia and the European Union in Cambodia. The second MoU covering the work under the PRACTISE project (Protecting Children Through System Strengthening and Evidence) and the NORAD project, funded by DFAT and the NORAD. Save the Children in Cambodia is implementing these projects in Phnom Penh, Sihanoukville, Kandal, Siem Reap, Battambang, Prey Veng and Tbong Khmum between 2019 – 2021.

[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Save the Children Signed MoU with MoSVY for Family Care First and REACT[:]

post.post_content: [:en] Phirum’s husband had a serious traffic accident, resulting in him being unable to work. This meant that she was the sole income earner for her family. Phirum was worried about her ability to provide for her chilldren and decided to seek advice from her village chief. The chief referred Phirum to Children In Families who have been able to support her daughters to stay with their parents. Family Care First, supported by USAID Cambodia and Save the Children Hong Kong, have prevented family separation by supporting 124 children in their families. Watch this short video to find out more. * Name has been changed for confidentiality.[:]
post.post_title: [:en]Preventing Unnecessary Family Separation[:]

post.post_content:
Meet Sokha, Dany and Sovat

Sokha, Dany and Sovat grew up in a slum area in Phnom Penh. They were placed in an orphanage when their parents were jailed for drug usage and theft.
With the support from USAID Cambodia and Save the Children Hong Kong, they were referred to a foster family supported by Hagar International. * Names have been changed for confidentiality.
post.post_title: Found a Home in Foster Care

post.post_content:
[:en]

Meet Sokha and Nita*

Nita has been living in a Residential Care Institution (RCI), Cambodian Organization for Living and Temporary-care, for more than 5 years. Now, COLT is transitioning into a community center after receiving support from Family Care First through M'luprussey Organization and ក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា To date, USAID Cambodia and Save the Children Hong Kong  have supported 21 RCIs to change their model of care to support children to live in families, instead of an institution. Children like Nita are being integrated to live with their families, where they can reach their full potential. Stay tuned for the full video. *Note: Names have been changed for confidentiality

Becoming a Community Center

Piseth has been a Residential Care Institution manager for many years. He used to think that Residential Care Institutions/Orphanages were a good alternative for children, until he started his master in Social Work and received additional trainings from Family Care First. Now, Piseth is supporting Nita* and other children at Cambodian Organization for Living and Temporary-care to return to their families where they can thrive.    [:kh]

ជួបជាមួយសុខា និងនីតា*

នីតាបានរស់នៅអង្គការខូលអស់រយៈពេលជាង៥ឆ្នាំមកហើយ។ ពេលនេះ អង្គការខូលកំពុងតែផ្លាស់ប្តូរពីមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារ ទៅជាមណ្ឌលគាំទ្រសហគមន៍វិញ ក្រោយពីបានទទួលការគាំទ្រពីអង្គការម្លប់ឫស្សី និងក្រសួងសង្គមកិច្ច អតីតយុទ្ធជន និងយុវនីតិសម្បទា។ មកទល់បច្ចុប្បន្ននេះ ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID និងអង្គការសង្គ្រោះកុមារហុងកុង បានគាំទ្រមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារចំនួន២១ ឱ្យមកគាំទ្រកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារវិញ ដែលធ្វើឱ្យកុមារ ដូចជានីតា ទទួលបានសក្តានុពលពេញលេញ។

ក្លាយទៅជាមណ្ឌលគាំទ្រសហគមន៍

ពិសិតជាប្រធានគ្រប់គ្រងមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារអស់រយៈពេលជាច្រើនឆ្នាំមកហើយ។ គាត់ធ្លាប់គិតថាមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារជាជម្រើសល្អ រហូតដល់ពេលដែលគាត់សិក្សាថ្នាក់អនុបណ្ឌិតផ្នែកសង្គមកិច្ច និងទទួលបានវគ្គបណ្តុះបណ្តាលបន្ថែមពីគម្រោងថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារជាអាទិភាពចម្បង។ ពេលនេះ ពិសិតកំពុងតែជួយនីតា* និងកុមារដទៃទៀតនៅអង្គការខូលឱ្យត្រឡប់ទៅរស់នៅជាមួយគ្រួសារពួកគេវិញ។ គម្រោងរបស់យើង ទទួលបានការគាំទ្រថវិកាពីទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID និងអង្គការសង្គ្រោះកុមារហុងកុង បានគាំទ្រពិសិត និងប្រធានមណ្ឌលដទៃទៀត តាមរយៈអង្គការម្លប់ឫស្សីដើម្បី ផ្តល់អាទិភាពដល់ការថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារវិញ។ *ឈ្មោះត្រូវបានផ្លាស់ប្តូរដើម្បីការពារអត្តសញ្ញាណកុមារ    [:]
post.post_title: [:en]From a Residential Care to a Community Center[:kh]ពីមណ្ឌលថែទាំកុមារកំព្រាទៅជាមណ្ឌលគាំទ្រសហគមន៍[:]

post.post_content:
[:en]

Meet Theara

This is the story of Theara, a one-year old boy who was abandoned at a hospital in Phnom Penh. Family Care First tried to trace his biological families, but it was not possible.

With support from Save the Children Hong Kong  and USAID Cambodia, Family Care First placed him in M'Lop Tapang foster family who has been taking good care of him. In 2017, we have placed 70 children like Theara into foster care.

* names have been changed for confidentiality.

[:kh]

ជួបជាមួយធារ៉ា

នេះគឺជាសាច់រឿងរបស់កុមារាឈ្មោះធារ៉ា ដែលត្រូវបានគេបោះបង់ចោលនៅមន្ទីរពេទ្យមួយ។ ក្រោមការគាំទ្ររបស់ទីភ្នាក់ងារជំនួយអាមេរិក USAID និងអង្គការសង្គ្រោះកុមារហុងកុង គម្រោងថែទាំកុមារក្នុងគ្រួសារជាអាទិភាពចម្បងបានទុកដាក់ធារ៉ា ជាមួយក្រុមគ្រួសារចិញ្ចឹមមួយ ដែលកំពុងបីបាច់ថែរក្សាគាត់បានយ៉ាងល្អ។

* ឈ្មោះត្រូវបានផ្លាស់ប្តូរដើម្បីការពារអត្តសញ្ញាណកុមារ។
